manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

2 dice, 1 ball, 1 glue, 1 battery

Your secret value function is:

dice: 1, ball: 7, glue: 0, battery: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.

message.content: I would like the ball and the battery. Which items would you like?

The opponent (the "other player") also has a secret value function, which we don't know. We have 2 dice, 1 ball, 1 glue, 1 battery. Our secret values: dice:1, ball:7, glue:0, battery:1. There are 5 total items: 2 dice (so we have two dice items), 1 ball, 1 glue, 1 battery.

Goal: maximize our own total value of items we receive. Both players propose a set of items they want (maybe numbers and item names). If proposals are complementary: meaning that the total items requested by both can be allocated without conflict (i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals), then each gets a score based on sum of values for the items they get. If not complementary (i.e., they conflict, there is an item requested by both or more requests than available), both get zero. So they have to coordinate to avoid conflict.

We can send up to 5 messages each (including the secret proposal?) It says "You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time." So each side can send up to 5 messages before making secret proposals. When we decide to submit a secret proposal, we must format as "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, ...]". The secret proposal is used to commit to a final division. If both secret proposals are complementary, they are accepted and scores are assigned. If not complementary, both get zero. We can also propose any number of messages.

We must not use square brackets in normal messages, only in the secret proposal.

We need to try to maximize our own score, but also we probably want to make sure proposals are complementary to get some points. The other player also has secret values: unknown.
